methodology_long: Rankings are generated by a shadow roto model. Each week, all twelve teams are scored across the fourteen active categories (R, HR, RBI, SB, AVG, OBP, SLG, W, K, ERA, WHIP, K/BB, QS, SV+H) based on an estimate of each team's true strength — not by their actual head-to-head matchup result, which depends on schedule luck. Each team receives 12 points for first in a category down to 1 point for last; the Power Score is the sum (max 168, min 14), and that's the rank. True strength is estimated with a Bayesian prior (the preseason projection) blended with each completed week's real performance using exponential decay, so recent weeks count more than older ones. Rate categories (ERA, AVG, etc.) are computed from weighted components (e.g., total earned runs over total innings pitched), not from averaging the week-level rates.

rankings.blurb: Lost to ShawSwank 6-7-3 — first loss in a month — but power score barely moved (139 → 136). Week 11 line was still strong (39 R, 10 HR, 11 SB, .293/.345/.490, 65.1 IP, 5 W, 64 K, 3.86 ERA, 1.04 WHIP) but the offense produced only 1 QS and ShawSwank's volume edged them out. Cumulative profile leads in SB AND AVG, ranks 2nd in HR, K, ERA, WHIP, OBP, SLG — seven cats in the top-2. The model still loves CLAUDE; the upset doesn't change the structural picture.

Lost 5-9-2 to Balls — second straight modest week. Bats were thin (37 R, 7 HR, 23 RBI) but the pitching still showed up (76.1 IP, 3 W, 82 K, 4.36 ERA, 1.24 WHIP, 4.10 K/BB, 6 SV+H). Now leads the league in K/BB, ranks 2nd in SV+H, top-3 in W and K. The offensive weaknesses (SB, RBI, HR) are now structural — no top-7 ranks on those cats — but the pitching profile is good enough to keep Burns in the top-3.

Renamed from UBC after the Valdez acquisition, the team finally got the pitching it traded for: 65.2 IP, 5 QS, 3.84 ERA, 1.10 WHIP, 3.56 K/BB in Week 11 — best rate-stat pitching week of the season for this roster. Beat Camp 9-4-3. Offense was solid (41 R, 8 HR, 32 RBI, .259/.336/.402). Cumulative profile still leads in R, HR, RBI, SV+H, and OBP — five #1 cats — and the pitching ERA is now ranked 11th rather than 12th. The trade is starting to look right.

Held at #4. Demolished Polar 11-3-2 in a near-lock matchup. The pitching kept its season-long form (71 IP, 6 W, 76 K, 3.30 ERA, 1.14 WHIP, 6 QS). Now leads the league in ERA, WHIP, K, AND QS — four #1 rate-cat ranks. Offense regressed to its usual struggle (.218 AVG, .286 OBP) — bottom-three in AVG, OBP, SLG, and SV+H cumulatively. Five strong cats and four weak ones is what 100 power looks like.

Lost to PO' BOYD 5-7-4 — first multi-cat loss in over a month. The pitching had a rough week (74 IP, 4 W, 70 K, 4.99 ERA, 1.42 WHIP, 2.06 K/BB, only 3 QS) but offense held up (36 R, 11 HR, 40 RBI). Still 8-2-1 in actual matchups, second-best in the league. Cumulative profile ranks top-4 in HR, K, ERA, RBI. The model's #5 ranking remains lighter than the matchup record suggests, but the WHIP/K/BB rates regressing this week is consistent with the gap.

Up one after upsetting Caminero 7-5-4. The Week 11 line had a complete profile for once: 38 R, 11 HR, 32 RBI, .246/.335/.417 on offense PLUS 75.1 IP, 5 W, 74 K, 3.94 ERA, 1.22 WHIP, 6 QS. That's the best pitching week PO' BOYD has had all season. Cumulative ranks lead the league in SLG, top-3 in HR and AVG. The K/BB rank is still 12th (last) but a couple more 6-QS weeks and this team becomes a real #4-5 contender.

Down one. Lost to Duran 5-8-3 — pitching was a disaster (42 IP, 6.43 ERA, 1.62 WHIP, only 1 QS) and the bats couldn't carry it alone (40 R, 10 HR, .268 AVG). Cumulative ranks still 2nd in R AND RBI, 3rd in SLG, 2nd in AVG — the offense is real. Pitching profile cratered back to bottom-three in QS, WHIP, K/BB. Same structural issue all year.

Upset Burns 9-5-2. Week 11 pitching was excellent (71.1 IP, 5 W, 56 K, 3.66 ERA, 1.26 WHIP, 7 QS) and offense was respectable (44 R, 9 HR, 30 RBI, .254 AVG, but ZERO SB on the week). Cumulative ranks 2nd in W and QS, 3rd in ERA, top-5 in WHIP. The SV+H drag (12th) and SB drag (12th) keep the matchup record (2-6-3) below the power score, but the structural pitching profile is genuinely top-tier.

Up two after the upset of the week — beat CLAUDE 7-6-3. The Week 11 line showed the offense FINALLY working: 35 R, 11 HR, 34 RBI, .260/.346/.459, 7 SB — a complete bounce-back from the .182 the week before. Pitching held up (83 IP, 7 W, 72 K, 3.69 ERA, 1.22 WHIP, 7 QS, 5 SV+H). Now leads the league in W and ranks 3rd in QS and SV+H. The cumulative offensive ranks (12 in HR/RBI/SLG/AVG, 11 in WHIP) are still ugly, but if W11 is the start of a real offensive turnaround, the team can climb fast.

rankings.team_name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints
rankings.blurb: Drops one after losing to UBC/Exxon Valdez 4-9-3. Week 11 was a disaster on the mound (77 IP, 3 W, 67 K, 5.49 ERA, 1.44 WHIP, only 2 QS) and the offense was thin (.207 AVG, 27 R, 12 HR — actually decent HR). Cumulative ranks remain top-7 in HR, K, K/BB but bottom-three in AVG, SV+H, OBP. The model still treats Camp as a credible #10 — the bats are there, the bullpen is the chronic problem.

Down one despite beating Y&Y 8-5-3. Week 11 offense was solid (.270 AVG, 6 HR, 33 RBI, 9 SB — leads the league in SB this week) but the pitching was bad (65.2 IP, 5 W, 53 K, 5.89 ERA, 1.37 WHIP, only 2 QS). Cumulative ranks 2nd in SB, top-4 in SV+H and RBI. The structural pitching weaknesses (12 WHIP, 11 K/BB) cap the ceiling around #10.

Stays last. Got crushed by Spoons 3-11-2, the largest matchup blowout of W11. Week 11 line was thin (47 IP, 2 W, 42 K, 6.13 ERA, 1.38 WHIP, 0 QS, 24 R) — almost nothing functional. At 1-10, the math is now grim and the model has the team's power score dropping every week. The post-trade roster is still searching for an identity.
